title: Action and Movement Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To run fast on four legs, like a horse.
choices: gallop
accelerate
spiral
rounds.answer: gallop
rounds.explanation: gallop: To run fast on four legs, like a horse.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To look at something for a long time.
choices: gallop
accelerate
gaze
rounds.answer: gaze
rounds.explanation: gaze: To look at something for a long time.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To feel pain or sadness.
choices: accelerate
suffer
gallop
rounds.answer: suffer
rounds.explanation: suffer: To feel pain or sadness.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To go faster, or to make something go faster.
rounds.answer: accelerate

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To look at something to make sure it is correct or safe.
rounds.answer: check

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To make someone feel very happy and interested.
rounds.answer: excite

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.answer: The horse began to gallop across the field.
The snail moved in a spiral on the ground.
She likes to gaze at the stars in the night sky.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

choices: A sign or message that tells you something.
To look at something for a long time.
To press something tightly with your hands.
To tell someone what something is like.
rounds.answer: To look at something for a long time.
rounds.explanation: She likes to gaze at the stars in the night sky.

choices: A small tool that makes a loud sound when you blow into it; also the sound.
To look at something to make sure it is correct or safe.
To press something tightly with your hands.
To tell someone what something is like.
rounds.answer: To look at something to make sure it is correct or safe.
rounds.explanation: Please check your homework before you turn it in.

choices: A sign or message that tells you something.
A small tool that makes a loud sound when you blow into it; also the sound.
The feeling of being pushed or stressed by something.
To press something tightly with your hands.
rounds.answer: A sign or message that tells you something.
rounds.explanation: The green light is a signal for us to go.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.target: The horse galloped quickly across the field.
Play with your AI tutor, a friend, or your family: One person acts out an action word from the Vocabulary list (squeeze, gallop, peek, whistle,...
Explain one important unit idea, give a reason with because, and respond to two follow-up questions.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Can you describe your favourite animal to me?

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Plants suffer when they have no water.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I will defend my friend if someone tries to bully him.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'signal' mean in this unit?
choices: A sign or message that tells you something.
To run fast on four legs, like a horse.
To go faster, or to make something go faster.
rounds.answer: A sign or message that tells you something.
rounds.explanation: signal: A sign or message that tells you something.

rounds.prompt: What does 'rescue' mean in this unit?
choices: To run fast on four legs, like a horse.
To go faster, or to make something go faster.
To save someone or something from danger.
rounds.answer: To save someone or something from danger.
rounds.explanation: rescue: To save someone or something from danger.

rounds.prompt: What does 'accelerate' mean in this unit?
choices: A shape that curls round and round, moving out from the centre.
To go faster, or to make something go faster.
To run fast on four legs, like a horse.
rounds.answer: To go faster, or to make something go faster.
rounds.explanation: accelerate: To go faster, or to make something go faster.
